title: Life in the UK starter mock
activity: Practice questions + starter mock mode + weak-topic review
next: Expand to a 24-question timed mock with full official-topic weighting and review mode.
progressionSteps: Start with civic basics and everyday UK facts
Practise weak citizenship topics from saved attempts

questions.prompt: Who appoints the Prime Minister after a general election?
questions.explanation: The monarch appoints as Prime Minister the person most likely to command the confidence of the House of Commons.

questions.prompt: Which part of Parliament is made up of elected MPs?
questions.explanation: Members of Parliament are elected to the House of Commons.

questions.prompt: Which document from 1215 is often linked with limits on royal power?
questions.explanation: Magna Carta was agreed in 1215 and is associated with limiting the power of the monarch.

questions.prompt: What is one responsibility of people living in the UK?
questions.explanation: Everyone living in the UK is expected to obey the law.

questions.prompt: What event is remembered on Remembrance Day?
options: The service and sacrifice of people in wars
The start of the school year
questions.explanation: Remembrance Day honours those who served and died in wars and conflicts.

questions.prompt: Which city is the capital of Wales?
questions.explanation: Cardiff is the capital city of Wales.

questions.prompt: At what age can most citizens vote in UK general elections?
questions.explanation: Most citizens can vote in UK general elections from age 18.

questions.prompt: Which service provides healthcare funded mainly through taxation?
questions.explanation: The National Health Service provides healthcare funded mainly through taxation.

questions.prompt: Which of these is one of the four countries of the UK?
questions.explanation: The UK is made up of England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland.

questions.prompt: Which flower is commonly associated with Remembrance?
questions.explanation: The poppy is used as a symbol of Remembrance.

questions.prompt: Where does the UK Parliament meet?
questions.explanation: The UK Parliament meets at the Palace of Westminster in London.

questions.prompt: What type of trial uses a jury in serious criminal cases?
questions.explanation: Serious criminal cases can be heard in the Crown Court with a jury.
